# R2 · Coordination & Consistency — Redis Patterns Applied

> Atomicity first: the foundation the queue chapters build on. Five coordination patterns — atomic updates, locking,
> Redlock (a contrast), cross-shard consistency, hash-tag co-location — plus a workshop, grounded in EchoMQ's real
> inline Lua transitions, the claim lease + `attempts` fencing token, and the braced `emq:{q}:` keyspace
> (echo/apps/echo_mq, v2), worked through the Exchange Platform. Served at /redis-patterns/coordination. A `soon`
> marker means the chapter is specified and on the way.

## Modules

- [R2.01 · Atomic updates](/redis-patterns/coordination/atomic-updates): read-modify-write without a race; every state move is one inline Lua script run EVALSHA-first (`EchoMQ.Jobs.enqueue`/`claim`). Dives: watch-multi-exec · lua-for-logic · shadow-key-bulk.
- [R2.02 · Distributed locking](/redis-patterns/coordination/distributed-locking): mutual exclusion via the claim lease (`ZADD active now+lease_ms`) with `attempts` (HINCRBY) as the fencing token; the generic SET NX PX is the contrast. Dives: set-nx-px · fencing-tokens · lease-renewal.
- [R2.03 · The Redlock algorithm](/redis-patterns/coordination/redlock): a majority-of-N multi-master lock, taught as a contrast with EchoMQ's single-Valkey claim lease. Dives: majority-of-n · clock-assumptions · single-instance-enough.
- [R2.04 · Cross-shard consistency](/redis-patterns/coordination/cross-shard-consistency): a multi-key Lua EVAL requires one slot, so the braced keyspace prevents a torn write; `attempts` is the monotone version token. Dives: torn-writes · version-tokens · commit-markers.
- [R2.05 · Hash-tag co-location](/redis-patterns/coordination/hash-tag-colocation): force related keys to one cluster slot — `EchoMQ.Keyspace.queue_key` → `emq:{q}:*` ("the hashtag IS the queue name"); `slot/1` = CRC16 % 16384, computed client-side. Dives: the-tag-mechanic · crossslot-prevention · cluster-auto-detect.
- [R2.06 · Workshop](/redis-patterns/coordination/workshop): make an Exchange Platform order placement atomic across runtimes — `Exchange.Gateway.parse_place/1` into the atomic `@enqueue` over co-located `emq:{orders}:*`. Door to the EchoMQ course.

## Up

- [Course home](/redis-patterns) · [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · next: R3 · Reliable Queues (soon)
